description: Pilot Hi-Tec-C. Tiny needle-tip gel pen, clean lines. The person buying one at Loft is either journaling beautifully or color-coding their entire nervous system.
Pilot Juice+. 0.4mm gel pen with a smooth Synergy Tip and soft white body. Good for notes, planners, and pretending your life has margins.
Pino. Six bite-sized vanilla ice creams wrapped in chocolate. Konbini freezer classic, eaten with the tiny pick like you have table manners.
PITTA MASK. Soft polyurethane mask, washable, and oddly stylish for something sold beside eye drops. Drugstore grab when pollen season starts bullying everyone.
Pizzaman. Steamed pizza bun with tomato sauce and stretchy cheese. Winter konbini counter food, best eaten immediately while walking like a gremlin.
DHC Placenta. Beauty supplement aisle item, usually tablets rather than glam drink fantasy. Drugstores sell it; labels matter because supplements are not candy.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: DHC Placenta, collagen drinks, placenta jelly, beauty supplement packs]; No reliable image source found

japanese_name: ミドリ 手帳シール
description: Midori planner stickers. Tiny Japan-made stickers for diaries and bullet journals. Loft and Hands bait for anyone who thinks one more sticker sheet will fix time management.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Midori diary stickers, schedule stickers, planner tabs, decorative sticker sheets]

english_name: Ganso Shokuhin Sample-ya Sample'n Cooking Kit
description: Sample'n Cooking food sample kit. DIY plastic food replica kit from Ganso Shokuhin Sample-ya. Buy it when staring at restaurant window displays turns into a personality.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Sample'n Cooking kit, fake ice cream workshop piece, plastic tempura sample, food sample magnet]

description: Daiso rain poncho. Cheap emergency plastic poncho. The no-style, all-survival move when Tokyo weather changes its mind.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Daiso rain poncho, adult rain poncho, disposable poncho, compact raincoat]

japanese_name: エアイン 富士山消しゴム
description: Plus Air-In Mt. Fuji Eraser. Eraser that turns into Mt. Fuji as you use it. Stationery souvenir for people who want their mistakes to become geography.
Plus PICO FRIENDS box cutter. Animal-shaped cardboard cutter with a ceramic blade. Cute enough for a desk, sharp enough to end the delivery-box struggle.
Pocari Sweat. Ion drink, mild grapefruit-ish flavor, Japan's default 'I am dehydrated' button. Konbini fridge staple after temples, trains, or questionable life choices.
Pocari Sweat versus Aquarius. Japan's blue-bottle sports drink rivalry. Pocari feels softer; Aquarius tastes sharper, both live in every konbini fridge.
notes: [POSSIBLE_DUPLICATE_OF: Pocari Sweat; AMBIGUOUS: Pocari Sweat, Aquarius]

input_name: Pocket WiFi Rental (Ninja WiFi, Sakura Mobile)
description: Ninja WiFi pocket router. Rental WiFi box for group trips. Pick it up at the airport, share it with everyone, then panic less about roaming.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Ninja WiFi pocket router, Sakura Mobile pocket WiFi, Japan Wireless pocket WiFi, Global Advanced Communications pocket WiFi]

japanese_name: サンリオ ポケットティッシュ
description: Sanrio pocket tissues. Tiny character tissue packs for bags, kids, and sudden shrine-side sneezes. Cheap, cute, and somehow more emotionally prepared than you.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Sanrio tissues, Pokémon tissues, character pocket tissue packs, kids tissue multipacks]

japanese_name: ポッキー / プリッツ
description: Pocky is chocolate sticks; Pretz is the savory cousin. Standard boxes are everywhere, regional flavors are suitcase bait.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Pocky Chocolate, Pocky regional flavors, Pretz Salad, Pretz regional flavors]

japanese_name: ポケモンデザインラボ カスタムTシャツ
description: Pokémon Design Lab custom T-shirt. Touchscreen station where you build a Pokémon tee and pick it up later. Shibuya Pokémon Center turns indecision into merch.

japanese_name: ポケモンセンター オリジナルグッズ
description: Pokémon Center original merch. Plush walls, local designs, and Japan-only chaos that triggers instant suitcase math. Budget room before entering.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: regional plush, Pokémon Center original stationery, exclusive apparel, local-design merchandise]

description: Zuikougama Kyoto pottery class. Spin clay near Yasaka Pagoda, then wait for firing and shipping like a patient adult. A calmer souvenir than another tote bag.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Zuikougama Kyoto class, Mashiko pottery experience, Tokoname Yamatane workshop, Shoraku Kiln Raku bowl class]

description: Ippodo Ummon matcha. Rich Kyoto matcha tin for serious tea people. Buy it when the shelves have not been raided by matcha TikTok.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Ippodo Ummon, Ippodo Sayaka, Marukyu Koyamaen Isuzu, Marukyu Koyamaen Wako]

english_name: Lawson Uchi Café Premium Roll Cake
japanese_name: プレミアムロールケーキ
description: Lawson Uchi Café Premium Roll Cake. Sponge ring with a huge cream center. Lawson's dessert fridge flex, eaten with a tiny spoon in your hotel room.
Soka senbei. Crisp soy-sauce rice crackers from Saitama. Old-school edible souvenir that survives suitcase abuse better than most snacks.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Soka senbei tin, Komiya Senbei gift box, assorted rice cracker tin, soy-sauce senbei assortment]

description: Kishibori soy sauce. Barrel-aged shoyu with a deep, clean salt hit. Bring it home when supermarket soy sauce starts feeling too basic.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Yuasa soy sauce, Kishibori Shoyu, Kamebishi soy sauce, premium barrel-aged shoyu]

japanese_name: ボス レインボーマウンテンブレンド
description: Boss Rainbow Mountain Blend. Canned coffee with milk, sugar, and serious vending-machine legacy. Grab it hot or cold when the train platform becomes breakfast.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Boss Rainbow Mountain Blend, Georgia Emerald Mountain Blend, UCC Black, Fire canned coffee]

description: PRESS BUTTER SAND. Crisp pressed butter cookie with buttercream and caramel inside. Tokyo Station gift-box energy, minus the fragile cake drama.
